title: buxus sinica subsp. aemulans
intro: Buxus sinica subsp. aemulans, also known as the Chinese boxwood or autumn boxwood, is a plant species within the genus Buxus, a group of evergreen shrubs or small trees. Traditionally, Buxus sinica subsp. aemulans has been used in Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Buxus sinica subsp. aemulans are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
